lessons.text: Pair knowledge with lived realization, not theory.
lessons.rationale: Verses 1-2 promise knowledge combined with realization, after which nothing remains to be known.

lessons.text: Accept that true realization is rare and hard-won.
lessons.rationale: Among thousands who strive, only one truly knows the essence.

lessons.text: Distinguish your body and mind from your living essence.
lessons.rationale: The eightfold material nature is inferior; the life-element is the higher nature that upholds the world.

lessons.text: Trace every beginning and ending to one source.
lessons.rationale: All beings arise from the two natures; all things are strung on the divine like gems on a thread.

lessons.text: Notice the divine in taste, light, and sound.
lessons.rationale: The divine is the flavor in water, light in sun and moon, fragrance in earth, life in all beings.

lessons.text: See the same essence behind every excellence.
lessons.rationale: The divine is the seed of all beings, intelligence of the intelligent, strength free of craving, desire aligned with dharma.

lessons.text: Recognize all states of being flow from one.
lessons.rationale: Pure, active, and inert states all proceed from the divine, yet it is not bound by them.

lessons.text: See past the three moods that veil reality.
lessons.rationale: The world is deluded by the three gunas; this divine illusion is crossed only by taking refuge.

lessons.text: Watch how delusion turns people from the good.
lessons.rationale: Those whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow lower ways and do not seek the divine.

lessons.text: Let your worship rise above distress and gain.
lessons.rationale: Four kinds seek the divine: the distressed, the curious, the wealth-seeker, and the wise.

lessons.text: Devote yourself with steady, single-minded love.
lessons.rationale: The wise, ever steadfast and devoted to the One, are dearest and held as the divine's own Self.

lessons.text: Realize the divine dwells within everything you see.
lessons.rationale: After many births the wise one realizes all this is the innermost Self; such a soul is rare.

lessons.text: Watch how desire steers you toward lesser aims.
lessons.rationale: Those whose wisdom is stolen by desire turn to lesser gods, led by their own nature.

lessons.text: Whatever you worship with faith is strengthened.
lessons.rationale: The divine makes firm whatever faith a devotee holds, and grants the outcome they seek.

lessons.text: Choose lasting aims over quick, finite rewards.
lessons.rationale: The reward of small minds is finite; devotees of the highest reach the highest.

lessons.text: Do not mistake the formless for mere form.
lessons.rationale: The foolish think the unmanifest is limited; the divine stays veiled by Yoga-Maya.

lessons.text: Remember the knower is never fully known.
lessons.rationale: The divine knows all beings past, present, and future, yet no one fully knows it.

lessons.text: Free yourself from the pull of like and dislike.
lessons.rationale: Beings fall into delusion through the pairs of opposites; the pure-hearted, freed from them, worship steadfastly.

lessons.text: Seek refuge to understand self, spirit, and action.
lessons.rationale: Those who take refuge realize Brahman, the Self, and all action, and know the divine even at death.
